headline: Endurance.
Resilience.
Propulsion.
blocks.subhead: Battle Road came together the honest way, through cheerful and relentless iteration, each pass keeping only the bits that worked.

blocks.label: The brief
heading: Distilled through
iteration.
paragraphs: Some ideas arrive fully formed. This one was earned, refined step by step until the core of it felt inevitable.

blocks.label: The idea
heading: Endurance,
mapped.
paragraphs: Early explorations circled endurance, resilience and a pioneering spirit drawn from the founders themselves. I worked through a range of directions before settling on the peregrine falcon, native to the company’s Boston headquarters and a rather good icon for an adventurous, fast-moving team.
Topographic lines felt like a natural fit too, suggesting aerial landscapes while nodding to the digital and the scientific.

blocks.text: The final mark captures a sense of propulsion, streamlined and inspired by navigational glyphs and the sharp geometry of flight paths. It suggests movement and clarity, balancing the raw with the precise.

blocks.caption: Topographic 3D landscapes of the Boston area, built in Blender.

blocks.label: The result
heading: Engineered
for speed.
paragraphs: The brand was designed for flexibility, as comfortable on a huge wall as it is in your hand on a business card. With a mix of 3D renders and vector linework, the client had enough to build on for years.

The website followed the same philosophy. Wireframes began intentionally brutalist, all harsh contrast and sharp angles, then resolved into something calmer, with clean grids, strong typography and measured negative space.
Motion mattered throughout. Menus slide, hovers reward a wandering cursor, transitions feel deliberate. The experience mirrors the identity, engineered, structured and built for speed.
